title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a guide giving information about a new city bus tour.
audio_script: Woman: Welcome to the information desk for the new city bus tour. This tour has been designed for visitors who want to see the city in a short time, but local people are welcome too. The tour begins outside the [14]theatre[/14], where there is plenty of space for people to wait safely. Please don’t wait by the station, because the bus only stops there on its way back. The full tour takes ninety minutes. The first important building you’ll pass is the old [15]bank[/15], which is now used as an art centre. Your guide will explain why it has such unusual windows. After that, the bus will go through the market area. You cannot get off there, but you’ll have time to take photos from the top deck. The only stop where passengers can leave the bus is at the [16]castle[/16]. You’ll have twenty minutes there before the bus continues. Tickets are cheaper if you buy them online, but you can also buy them from the driver. A child ticket costs [17]five[/17] pounds. Adults pay eight pounds. The bus has recorded information in three languages, but live information will be given in [18]English[/18] only. If it rains, the top of the bus can be covered, so the tour will still run. The last tour of the day leaves at [19]4:40[/19]. After that, the bus is used for school transport. We hope you enjoy discovering the city from a different view. Please arrive ten minutes before the tour, especially if you need help getting upstairs or want to sit near the front.

items.text_before: Old building now used as art centre

questions.explanation: The tour begins outside the theatre.

questions.prompt: Old building now used as art centre
questions.explanation: The old bank is now used as an art centre.
The only stop where passengers can leave the bus is at the castle.
A child ticket costs five pounds.
Live information will be given in English only.
